steps: Go to https://analytics.amplitude.com/settings/projects and sign in to your Amplitude account (or create a free account at https://amplitude.com/)
Click on the project you want to use, or create a new project
On the project settings page, scroll down to find your 'API Key' and 'Secret Key'
Copy the API Key and Secret Key
To use the HTTP API (ingestion), use the API key directly in the request body
To use Dashboard/Export REST APIs, combine API key and secret key as Basic auth credentials (Base64 encode 'api_key:secret_key')

credential_fields.name: api_key
credential_fields.label: API Key
credential_fields.description: Your Amplitude project API key. Used for event tracking and as the username in Basic auth for analytics APIs.

notes: Amplitude uses two credentials: API Key (public) and Secret Key (private)
For dashboard/export/taxonomy APIs: Basic auth = Base64('api_key:secret_key') in Authorization header
For the event ingestion API (HTTP V2): API key goes in the request body JSON as 'api_key' field
Free Starter plan available with unlimited events for up to 1M monthly tracked users
Each Amplitude project has its own unique API key and secret key
Secret key can be regenerated in project settings if compromised

test_endpoint.description: List all event types in the project (requires Basic auth with api_key:secret_key)
